creator: riggs@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Dimitry Andric from comment #20)

> * thread #1: tid = 100600, 0x01a17022 mplayer, name = 'mplayer', stop reason
> = hardware error
>     frame #0: 0x01a17022 mplayer
> -> 0x1a17022:  vmovdqa (%esi,%ecx), %xmm0
>    0x1a17027:  vmovdqa (%esi,%ecx,2), %xmm1
> [...]
> E.g. you can clearly see that is trying to move data which is not 16-byte
> aligned to xmm0: %esi is 0xbfbfcae8 and %ecx is 0x00000010.
> 
> I'm not sure where these instructions come from, but I would bet they are
> from assembly source compiled with yasm, with the assumption of an incoming
> stack alignment of 16 bytes.

These two vmovdqa come from ffmpeg/libavfilter/x86/af_volume.asm:88,89, so you are right. This is compiled with yasm.
